hadiths.ar:
4004  -  ورَّوَّى ع ِي َّاب ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم س َّلاَّمُّ كَّزِةَّ ن َّي ْغَّ 
إَّلل َّحْمِ ن ِالْحَّي َّوَّإن ِ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Umar ibn Yazid, the seller of Sabiri, who said: I said to Abu 
Abdullah {a.s}: "May I be your ransom! People claim that making a profit from someone 
in distress is forbidden and is considered usury." 
Imam {a.s} said: "Have you ever seen anyone, whether rich or poor, buy something 
except out of necessity, O Umar? Indeed, 'Allah {SWT} has permitted trade and forbidden 
usury.' So make a profit, but do not commit usury." 
I said: "What is usury?" 
Imam {a.s} said: "It is exchanging dirhams for dirhams of equal amount."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.278 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.18 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.3 
p.72 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.459 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.447 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol.1 p.553 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4003
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠٠٣

hadiths.ar:
4005 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّ رَّج ُّل  إَّلص َّاذِق َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝  ي َّمْحَّق ُّ إَّللّٰهُّ إَّلزِّن ٰا وَّ ي ُّزْن ِى  إَّلص َّذَّق ٰاب ِ ۝ 
وَّ ق َّذْ إ َّرَّى مَّن ْ ي َّا ْكُّلُّ إَّلزِّن َّا ي َّزْن ُّو مَّالُّهُّ ق َّق َّال ي َّمْحَّق ُّ إَّلذِّي ن َّ ق َّا ِن ْ ي َّاب َّ مِن ْهُّ ذ َّهَّث َّ مَّالُّهُّ وَّ 
إِق ْي َّق َّز

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4004
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠٠٤

hadiths.ar:
4006 - وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن َّان   عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ عَّلِى ٍّ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ وَّ جَّم َّاذِ ن ْن ِ عُّي ْمَّان َّ عَّن ْ عُّن َّي ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ عَّلِى ٍّ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْ ث ُّ إ َّن َّا 
عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول اءِ ي َّي َّق َّاص َّلُّ ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ ن ِن َّي ْعِهِ 
مِي ْلَّي ْن ِ ن ِمِي ْلٍ ي َّذإً ن ِي َّذٍ ق َّا َّم َّا ن َّط ِزَّة ً ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ لأَّ ي َّصْلُّح
hadiths.en:
A man asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about the saying of 
Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic:  
"Allah {SWT} destroys usury and gives increase for charities." (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:276).  
He asked: “Yet I see those who consume usury, and their wealth increases."  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Is there any destruction greater than a dirham of usury that destroys 
one's religion? If he repents from it, his wealth will vanish, and he will become poor."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.279 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Vol.1 p.293 • Tafsir Kanz 
Al-Daqaiq, Vol.2 p.456 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4005
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠٠٥

hadiths.ar:
4007 - وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ذَّر َّإح ٍ عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال وَّ إَّلذ َّإن  َّه ُّ ن ِالذ َّإن  َّي َّي ْن ِ 
ي َّذإً ن ِي َّذٍ لَّي ْسَّ ن ِهِ ن َّا ْس ن ِالي  َّوْب ِ ن ِالي  َّوْن َّي ْن ِ ي َّذإً ن ِي َّذٍ وَّ ن َّسِي ن َّه ً إ ِذ َّإ وَّصَّق ْي َّهُّمَّا
hadiths.en:
Aban narrated from Muhammad ibn Ali al -Halabi and Hammad ibn Uthman, 
from Ubaydullah ibn Ali al-Halabi, who said:  
I heard Abu Abdullah {a.s} say: 
"There is no harm in selling different types of food, goods, or items that vary in value, 
two for one, if it is done hand -to-hand. However, if it is done on credit, then it is not 
permissible." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.191 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.279 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.93 
• Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.93 • Awali Al -La’ali, Vol.3 p.220 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.592 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, 
Vol.18 p.145 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.157 • Al-Fusul Al-Muhimmah, Vol.2 p.254 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4006
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠٠٦

hadiths.ar:
4008  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ْ ن َّي ْغِ إَّلْحَّي َّوَّإن ِ إِي ْن َّي ْن ِ ن ِوَّإجِذٍ -  ق َّق َّال 
ن َّا ْس
hadiths.en:
Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Zurarah, from Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad ibn 
Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, who said: "There is no harm in exchanging a camel for two camels or 
an animal for two animals, as long as it is done hand-to-hand." 
And Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in exchanging one garment for two garments, 
whether hand-to-hand or on credit, if you describe them." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.279 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.590 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.18 
p.155 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4007
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠٠٧

hadiths.ar:
4009  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّن ْذ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلْعَّن ْذِ ن ِالْعَّن ْذَّي ْن ِ وَّ إَّلْعَّ ن ْذِ ن ِالْعَّن ْذِ وَّ 
إَّلذ َّرَّإهِمِ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Sama'ah asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about selling two animals for one.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If you specify their age, then there is no harm." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.279 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4008
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠٠٨

hadiths.ar:
4010  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ سَّعِي ذُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّسَّارٍ : عَّن ِ إَّلْن َّعِي زِ ن ِالْن َّعِي زَّي ْن ِ ي َّذإً ن ِي َّذٍ وَّ ن َّسِي ن َّه ً ق َّق َّال 
ج َّذ َّعَّان َّ إ َّوْ ي َّن ِي  َّان َّ، ي ُّم َّ إ َّمَّزَّن ِى  ق َّح َّطَّطْث ُّ عَّلَّى إَّلن  َّسِي ن َّه ِ لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلن  َّاسَّ ي َّق ُّولُّون َّ لأَّ وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ق َّعَّلَّ  ذ َّلِك َّ لِلي  َّق ِي  َّه ِ
hadiths.en:
Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about exchanging 
one slave for two slaves, or one slave for another slave along with dirhams.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in exchanging all types of animals hand -to-hand." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.191 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.280 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 
p.118 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.3 p.100 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.591 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.156 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4009
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠٠٩

hadiths.ar:
4011  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن َّان   عَّن ْ سَّلَّمَّه َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّم 
ن ِالْعِزَّإق ِ ق َّكَّان َّ ق ِى  إَّلْكِسْوَّة ِ جُّل َّه   ج َّي ِّذَّة   ق َّسَّا َّلَّهُّ إ ِي  َّاهَّا إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّا َّن َّى ق َّق َّالَّ  إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّ لاَّم
إ ُّعْطِي ك َّ مَّكَّان َّهَّا جُّل َّي َّي ْن ِ لْحُّل َّه َّ وَّ ج َّعَّلَّ إَّلْحُّلَّلَّ ق ِى   
جَّح ْزِةِ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Sa'id ibn Yasar asked him about exchanging one camel for two camels, 
whether hand-to-hand or on credit.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, there is no harm if their ages are specified, whether they are young 
or mature." 
Then Imam {a.s} instructed me to strike out the mention of credit because people say it 
is not allowed, and Imam {a.s} only did that as a precaution (taqiyyah). 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.280 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4010
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠١٠

hadiths.ar:
4012  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي ل  عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ن ِالذ َّق ِي ق ِ مِي ْلاً 
ن ِمِي ْلٍ لأَّ ن َّا ْسَّ ن ِهِ
hadiths.en:
Aban narrated from Salamah, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} that: 
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} distributed clothing among the people in Iraq, and among the 
garments was a fine robe. Imam Hussain ibn Ali {a.s} asked him for it, but Imam Ali ibn 
Abi Talib {a.s} refused. 
Imam Hussain ibn Ali {a.s} then said: "I will give you two robes in exchange for it."  
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} refused. 
Imam Hussain ibn Ali {a.s} continued offering until he reached five robes. Imam Ali ibn 
Abi Talib {a.s} then took the robes from him, gave him the fine robe, and placed the robes 
in his lap, saying: "I will take five in exchange for one."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.280 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.119 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 
p.596 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.154 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4011
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠١١

hadiths.ar:
4013  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ن ِزَّإ ْسٍ لأَّ ي ُّز َّإذُّ وَّإجِذ  مِن ْهُّمَّا  
عَّلَّى إَّلْأ ج َّزِ
hadiths.en:
Jamil narrated from Zurarah, from Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -
Baqir {a.s}, who said: "Flour in exchange for wheat, and parched flour in exchange for 
regular flour, if it is equal in measure, there is no harm in it." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.189 • Da’a’im Al-Islam, Vol.2 p.42 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.280 
• Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.94 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.580 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.580 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 
p.141 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.142 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.13 p.340 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4012
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠١٢

hadiths.ar:
4014 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلط َّعَّامِ و إَّلي  َّمْزِ وَّ إَّلز  َّن ِي ث ِ ق َّق َّال 
ن َّوْعٍ إ ِلَّى ن َّوْعٍ إ ج َّزَّ ق َّا ِذ َّإ صَّزَّق ْي َّهُّ ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ ن ِهِ إِي ْن َّان ِ ن ِوَّإجِذٍ وَّ إ َّكْي َّزَّ مِن ْ ذ َّلِك
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir narrated  from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq 
{a.s}, who said: "Wheat and barley should be exchanged one for one, and neither of them 
should exceed the other." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.187 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.281 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.95 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.577 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.138 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4013
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠١٣

hadiths.ar:
4015  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ ق َّي ْسٍ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْث ُّ إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول 
ن ِوَّسْق َّي ْن ِ مِن ْ ي َّمْزِ ج َّي ْن َّزَّ لِأ َّن  َّ ي َّمْزَّ إَّلْمَّذِي ن َّه ِ إ َّج ْوَّذُّهُّمَّا طَّث ِ عَّاج ِلاً ن ِمِي ْلِ كَّي ْلِهِ إ ِلَّى إ َّج َّلٍ  
مِن ْ إ َّج ْلِ إ َّن  َّ إَّلز ُّطَّث َّ ي َّي ْن َّسُّ ق َّي َّن ْق ُّضُّ مِن ْ كَّي ْلِهِ
hadiths.en:
Sama'ah asked him about food, dates, and raisins.  
Imam {a.s} said: "It is not permissible to exchange two for one of any of these, except if 
you change it from one type to another. When you change it, then there is no harm in 
exchanging two for one or even more than that." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.281 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.95 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 
p.584 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.146 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4014
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠١٤

hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays, who said: I heard Abu Ja’far Imam 
Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} say: "It is disliked to exchange one wasq of dates from 
Medina for two wasqs of dates from Khaybar, because the dates of Medina are of 
better quality." 
Imam {a.s} also said: "It is disliked to sell dates in exchange for fresh dates, even if 
measured equally, with a delayed payment, because fresh dates dry out and decrease 
in volume." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.281 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.584 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4015
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠١٥

hadiths.ar:
4017   -  و-   سَّا َّلَّ ذَّإوُّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحُّصَّي ْن ِ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلس  َّاة ِ ن ِالس  َّاي َّي ْن ِ وَّ إَّلْن َّي ْص َّه ِ ن ِ الْن َّي ْص َّي َّي ْن ِ ق َّال 
ن َّا ْسَّ مَّا لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ مَّكِي لاً إ َّوْ مَّوْر ُّون اً
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} about a 
man who gave his servant ten dirhams on the condition that the servant would pay 
back ten dirhams each month.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in that." 
[REFERENCES] Masa’il Ali Ibn Ja’far, Vol.1 p.125 • Qurb Al-Isnad, Vol.1 p.266 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, 
Vol.3 p.281 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.28 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 p.157 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4016
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠١٦

hadiths.ar:
4018  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال مَّا لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ كَّي ْلاً وَّ لأَّ 
وَّر ْن اً
hadiths.en:
Dawud ibn al -Husayn asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about exchanging one sheep 
for two sheep and one egg for two eggs.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm, as long as it is not something measured or weighed." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.281 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4017
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠١٧

hadiths.ar:
4019   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لَّهُّ ي َّح ِي ن ُّن ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّل ي َّطْلُّث ُّ ن َّي ْغَّ إَّلْحَّزِي زِ  
مِن ِّى  وَّ  لَّي ْسَّ عِن ْذِى  مِن ْهُّ س َّى ْء  ق َّي ُّق َّاوِلُّن ِى  وَّ إ ُّق َّاوِلُّهُّ ق ِى  إَّلزِّن ْحِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّج َّلِ جَّي  َّى ن َّح ْي َّمِغَّ عَّلَّى س َّى ْءٍ ي ُّم َّ  إ َّذ ْهَّث ُّ ق َّا َّس ْي َّزِى  
لَّهُّ وَّ إ َّذْعُّوةُّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ ق َّق َّال سْي َّطِي غُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ْصَّزِف َّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ وَّ ي َّذَّعَّك َّ إ َّوْ 
وَّج َّذْب َّ إ َّن ْث َّ ذ َّلِك َّ إ َّ ي َّسْي َّطِي غُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ْصَّزِف َّ عَّن ْهُّ وَّ ي َّذَّعَّه
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq 
{a.s} that Imam {a.s} said:  
"There is no harm in exchanging goods, as long as they are not measured or weighed." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.282 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.595 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.18 
p.155 • Al-Fusul Al-Muhimmah, Vol.2 p.254 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4018
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠١٨

hadiths.ar:
4020  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ إ َّن ُّو إَّلص َّن  َّاحِ إَّلْكِن َّان ِى  ُّ : عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إِس ْي َّزَّى مِن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ مِان َّه َّ مَّن ٍّ صُّق ْزإً ن ِكَّذ َّإ وَّ كَّذ َّإ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ عِن ْذَّةُّ مَّا  
إُّس ْي ُّزِى َّ مِن ْهُّ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated  from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}:  
I said to Imam {a.s}, "A man comes to me asking to buy silk, but I do not have any. We 
negotiate about profit and payment terms until we agree on something. Then I go and 
buy it for him and invite him to complete the transaction."  
Imam {a.s} said: "What if he finds another deal that he prefers over what you have, can 
he leave you and go for it? Or if you find a better deal, can you leave him and pursue 
it?" 
I said: “Yes." 
Imam {a.s} said: "Then there is no harm." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.200 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.282 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.50 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.699 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.50 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 p.137 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4019
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠١٩

hadiths.ar:
4021   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحَّح  َّاح ِ : عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّس ْي َّزِى  إَّلط َّعَّامَّ مِن َّ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ لَّي ْسَّ عِن ْذَّةُّ وَّ ي َّس ْي َّزِى  مِن ْهُّ جَّالأًّ 
ق َّال  لأَّ ي َّزَّوْن َّ ق ِي هِ ن َّا ْساً  
ي َّق ُّولُّون َّ  هَّذ َّإ إ ِلَّى إ َّج َّلٍ ق َّا ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ إ ِلَّى ع َّي ْزِ إ َّج َّلٍ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ هُّوَّ عِن ْذَّ صَّاجِن ِهِ ق َّلاَّ ي َّصْلُّحُّ ق َّق َّال ن ْ إ َّج َّل  كَّان َّ إ َّجَّق  َّ  
ن ِهِ  إ َّج َّلٍ وَّ جَّالأًّ لأَّ ي ُّسَّمِّى  لَّهُّ إ َّج َّلاً إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ 
ي َّكُّون َّ ن َّي ْعاً لأَّ ي ُّوج َّذُّ مِي ْلُّ إَّلْعِن َّث ِ وَّ إَّلْن ِطِّي ح ِ وَّ س ِن ْهِهِ ق ِى  ع َّي ْزِ ر َّمَّان ِهِ ق َّلاَّ ي َّن ْن َّع ِى  س ِزَّإءُّ ذ َّلِك َّ  جَّالأ
hadiths.en:
Abu al -Sabbah al -Kinani asked Imam {a.s} about a man who bought one 
hundred mann of brass for a specified price, while the seller did not possess the 
quantity sold at the time of the transaction. 
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm as long as he delivers the specified weight that was 
agreed upon." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.282 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.701 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4020
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠٢٠

hadiths.ar:
4022  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ ق َّي ْسٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلا م
ق َّق َّالَّ إ ِن  َّ ي َّمَّن َّهَّا كَّذ َّإ وَّ كَّذ َّإ ي َّذإً ن ِي َّذٍ وَّ ي َّمَّن ُّهَّا كَّذ َّإ وَّ كَّذ َّإ ن َّط ِزَّة ً ق َّح ُّذ ْهَّا ن ِا َّى ِّ ي َّمَّن ٍ س ِن ْث َّ وَّ إِج ْعَّلْ صَّق ْق َّي َّهَّا وَّإجِذَّة ً ق َّالَّ
hadiths.en:
Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj asked Imam {a.s} about a man who buys food from 
another man who does not have it in his possession and buys it immediately (without 
delay). 
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it." 
I said: “But they consider it invalid among us." 
Imam {a.s} said: "What do they say about salam (forward contracts)?" 
I said: “They see no harm in it because it is for a specified term. However, if it is without 
a specified term and not in the seller’s possession, they say it is invalid."  
Imam {a.s} said: "If there is no specified term, then it is even more justified."  
Then Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in a man buying food that is not in the seller’s 
possession, whether on credit or immediately, without specifying a term, unless it is 
for items that are not commonly available, such as grapes, melons, and similar goods 
outside their season. In such cases, it is not proper to buy them immediately."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.282 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.49 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 
p.702 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.46 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
«

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4021
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠٢١

hadiths.ar:
4023  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم إ َّمَّزَّةُّ ن َّق َّز  إ َّن ْ ي َّن ْي َّاعَّ لَّهُّمْ ن َّعِي زإً ن ِوَّرِق ٍ وَّ ي َّز ِي ذُّون َّهُّ ق َّوْق َّ ذ َّلِك َّ ن َّط ِزَّة ً 
ق َّان ْي َّاعَّ لَّهُّمْ ن َّعِي زإً وَّ مَّعَّهُّ ن َّعْص ُّهُّمْ ق َّمَّن َّعَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّا ْج ُّذ َّ مِن ْهُّمْ ق َّوْق َّ وَّرِق ِهِ ن َّط ِزَّة ً
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Qays narrated from Abu Ja’far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -
Baqir {a.s} that Commander of the Faithful {a.s} said: 
"Whoever sells a commodity and says, 'Its price is such and such if paid immediately, 
and its price is such and such if paid later,' then take it at whichever price you wish and 
make it a single transaction." 
Then Imam {a.s} said: "He is only entitled to the lower price, even if it is on credit."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.283 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 84, H. 4022
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٤، ح ٤٠٢٢
